sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, William, presented with a history of injuring his right knee at a trampoline park a week ago, which he describes as a sudden pain and a "pop" when he landed.
He has been experiencing pain on the outside of the knee, which has been managed with Tylenol but not significantly improved.
He reports a slight limp and difficulty walking.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension, for which he is currently taking 20mg of lisinopril daily.
He also has diabetes, for which he is taking metformin 500mg daily, but his diet and exercise regimen are inconsistent.
He reports checking his blood sugar levels 4-5 times a week.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 180/80
Pulse rate: [NOT MENTIONED]
Oxygen saturation: [NOT MENTIONED]
Temperature: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's blood pressure was noted to be high.
The heart rate and rhythm were normal, and no murmur was appreciated.
The lungs sounded clear bilaterally.
The knee exam revealed bruising and swelling (ecchymosis and edema) along the lateral aspect of the knee, but no effusion.
The patient had a slight limp and pain with movement, but a decent range of motion.
Test Results: The patient's right knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Right knee pain; hypertension; diabetes; suspected medication non-adherence.
Plan:
I prescribed Meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling associated with the right knee injury.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I continued the patient's lisinopril at 20mg daily and ordered an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function and potential medication modification.
I also ordered an A1C and lipid panel to assess the patient's diabetes management.
Follow-up: I instructed the patient to follow up with me after receiving the results of the ordered tests.
